briefdescription.para: This function is a stub for the asynchronous callback.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layer forgot to set the handlers. On initialization, all handlers are set to this callback. It is considered an error for this handler to be invoked.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the HDMI RX core.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the HDMI RX core. Initialization of the HDMI RX includes setting up the instance data, and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmiRx core instance.
points to the configuration structure associated with the HDMI RX core.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if HDMI RX PIO ID mismatched.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the HDMI RX variables and sets them to the defaults.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

simplesect.para: This is required after a reset or init.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the HDMI RX stream parameters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

specifies the pixel per clock.
1 = XVIDC_PPC_1
2 = XVIDC_PPC_2
4 = XVIDC_PPC_4
specifies reference pixel clock frequency.

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI RX Internal VRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI RX Internal VRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI RX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI Internal video reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx_INT_VRST(XV_HdmiRx *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI RX Internal LRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI RX Internal LRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI RX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI Internal link reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx_INT_VRST(XV_HdmiRx *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI RX External VRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI RX External VRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI RX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI external video reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx_EXT_VRST(XV_HdmiRx *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function asserts or releases the HDMI RX External SYSRST.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either assert or release HDMI RX External SYSRST.

simplesect.para: The reset output of the PIO is inverted. When the system is in reset, the PIO output is cleared and this will reset the HDMI RX. Therefore, clearing the PIO reset output will assert the HDMI External system reset. C-style signature: void XV_HdmiRx_EXT_SYSRST(XV_HdmiRx *InstancePtr, u8 Reset)

briefdescription.para: This function sets the pixel rate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the color format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables/clear Hot-Plug-Detect.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.
specifies TRUE/FALSE value to either enable or clear HPD respectively.

briefdescription.para: This function write data to SCDC register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.
specifies the SCDC register address to write to.
specifies the data to be written.

simplesect.para: SCDC register 0x01,0x02,0x10,0x20,0x21,0x40 are handled by H/W directly. These registers should not be written by User application.

briefdescription.para: This function provides status of the HDMI RX core Link Status peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

specifies one of the type for which status to be provided:
0 = Link error counter for channel 0.
1 = Link error counter for channel 1.
2 = Link error counter for channel 2.

simplesect.para: Link status of the HDMI RX core link.

briefdescription.para: This function provides status of one of the link error counters reached the maximum value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

listitem.para: FALSE = Maximum error counter not reached.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the link error counters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function provides audio clock regenerating CTS (Cycle-Time Stamp) value at the HDMI sink device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function provides audio clock regenerating factor N value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the size of the EDID buffer of the DDC slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function loads the EDID data into the DDC slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.
is a pointer to the EDID data array.
is the length, in bytes, of the EDID array.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the EDID data was loaded successfully
XST_FAILURE if the EDID data load failed

briefdescription.para: This function sets the HDCP address in the DDC peripheral.

detaileddescription.para: This is implemented as a function and not a macro, so the HDCP driver can bind the function call with a handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.
is the HDCP address.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XHdmiRx_DdcHdcpSetAddress(XHdmi_Rx *InstancePtr, u8 Address)

briefdescription.para: This function writes HDCP data in the DDC peripheral.

detaileddescription.para: This is implemented as a function and not a macro, so the HDCP driver can bind the function call with a handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.
is the HDCP data to be written.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XHdmiRx_DdcHdcpWriteData(XHdmi_Rx *InstancePtr, u8 Data)

briefdescription.para: This function reads HDCP data from the DDC peripheral.

detaileddescription.para: This is implemented as a function and not a macro, so the HDCP driver can bind the function call with a handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

simplesect.para: Returns the HDCP data read from the DDC peripheral.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the number of bytes of the HDCP 2.2 write buffer in the DDC slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the status of the HDCP 2.2 write buffer in the DDC slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE = HDCP 2.2 message buffer is empty.
FALSE = HDCP 2.2 message buffer contains data.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the number of bytes of the HDCP 2.2 read buffer in the DDC slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the status of the HDCP 2.2 read message buffer in the DDC slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XHdmi_Rx core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE = HDCP 2.2 message buffer is empty.
FALSE = HDCP 2.2 message buffer contains data.

briefdescription.para: This function prints stream and timing information on STDIO/Uart console.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function provides status of the stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function provides the stream scrambler status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function provides the stream connected status.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the SCDC TMDS clock ratio bit.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE = TMDS clock ratio bit is set.
FALSE = TMDS clock ratio bit is cleared.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the AVI VIC (captured by the AUX peripheral)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the AVI colorspace (captured by the AUX peripheral)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the GCP color depth (captured by the AUX peripheral)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the divider for the frame calculation.

parameterdescription.para: is the dividend value to use in the calculation.
is the divisor value to use in the calculation.

briefdescription.para: This function searches for the video mode based on the vic.

simplesect.para: Vic defined in the VIC table.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the video properties from the aux peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the video timing from the VTD peripheral.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XV_HdmiRx core instance.

briefdescription.para: This is the main file for Xilinx HDMI RX core.

detaileddescription.para: Please see xv_hdmirx.h for more details of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 gm, mg 11/03/15 Initial release. 1.01 MG 30/12/15 Added DDC HDCP 2.2 calls 1.02 yh 15/01/16 Added 3D Video support 1.03 MG 19/02/16 Added link error callback 1.04 MG 08/03/16 Updated XV_HdmiRx_SetStream to use RefClk 1.05 MG 27/05/16 Updated XV_HdmiRx_CfgInitialize 1.06 YH 18/07/16 Replace xil_printf with xdbg_printf 1.07 YH 25/07/16 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for BaseAddress XV_HdmiRx_CfgInitialize 1.08 YH 18/08/16 squash unused variable compiler warning 1.09 YH 29/08/16 Set Match to FALSE when HTotal = 0 1.10 MG 02/03/17 Fixed YUV420 reading in function XV_HdmiRx_GetVideoTiming 1.40 YH 19/07/17 Clean up Print Statement line ending to "\r\n" 05/09/17 Enhanced Video Timing checking 2.00 YH 16/11/17 Added dedicated reset for each clock domain YH 16/11/17 Added bridge overflow interrupt EB 18/01/18 Moved VicTable to Hdmi Common library EB 26/01/18 Updated XV_HdmiRx_GetVideoTiming to use XVidC_GetVideoModeIdExtensive 2.20 EB 16/08/18 Replaced TIME_10MS, TIME_16MS, TIME_200MS with XV_HdmiRx_GetTime10Ms, XV_HdmiRx_GetTime16Ms XV_HdmiRx_GetTime200Ms Added TMDS Clock Ratio callback support 2.30 EB 05/03/19 Updated the description of the API XV_HdmiRx_GetLinkStatus 2.40 EB 30/07/19 Updated XV_HdmiRx_GetVideoTiming to fix an issue where FrameRate for YUV420 may be incorrect Updated XV_HdmiRx_GetVideoTiming to fix an issue where video timing values may be incorrect 06/08/19 Added Vic and Video Timing mismatch callback support 2.50 EB 02/12/18 Added 3D Audio Support
